instructions.name: accept_operator
docs: Two-step operator transfer, step 2 — the proposed successor accepts, proving key
control. Prevents fat-fingering control to an unspendable address.

instructions.name: cancel_pipeline
docs: Consumer cancels a pipeline that has no claimed/settled nodes, recovering
the full vault. Node accounts (remaining_accounts) are closed to consumer.

instructions.name: claim_node
docs: An agent claims a node once all dependencies are settled and its tier is
sufficient. CPIs into bonded_registry to increment the open-job counter.

instructions.name: complete_node
docs: Facilitator settles a claimed node: pays the agent (minus fee), pays the
operator fee, decrements the open-job counter, and writes reputation.

instructions.name: create_pipeline
docs: Create a DAG pipeline, lock the full budget into a vault, and create one
PipelineNode account per node (passed as remaining_accounts in order).

instructions.name: dispute_node
docs: The consumer challenges a submitted node within the dispute window.
`reason_code`: 0 = HashMismatch, 1 = Unavailable, 2 = IncorrectOutput.
Codes 0/1 are objectively checkable against `uri`+`result_hash`; 2 is
subjective and resolves via the arbiter. Recorded for triage only.

instructions.name: expire_node
docs: Permissionless expiry of an overdue node. Cascades expiry to all
downstream (still-pending) nodes and refunds the consumer in one tx.
If the target node was claimed, slashes its agent and records a failure.

instructions.name: finalize_node
docs: Permissionless finalize after the dispute window elapses with no dispute:
pays the agent (minus fee) + operator fee and records completion reputation.

instructions.name: initialize
docs: One-time operator setup. Stores the fee, the facilitator authority
(permitted to settle nodes) and the canonical dag_authority PDA bump.

instructions.name: migrate_pipeline_config
docs: One-time migration that grows a pre-hardening PipelineConfig to the current layout
and seeds the new fields with safe defaults. Idempotent (rejects if already migrated).

instructions.name: propose_operator
docs: Two-step operator transfer, step 1 — current operator proposes a successor
(e.g. a Squads multisig). No effect until the successor calls accept_operator.

instructions.name: resolve_dispute
docs: Arbiter (facilitator authority, v1) resolves a disputed node. Upheld →
refund the consumer + slash the agent + record a failure. Rejected →
settle as normal (pay the agent, record completion).

instructions.name: set_dispute_window
docs: Operator tunes the dispute window (slots), bounded. Applies to NEW submissions only —
in-flight nodes keep the window snapshotted at their submit time.

instructions.name: set_facilitator_authority
docs: Operator rotates the facilitator authority (key rotation / decentralization).

instructions.name: set_paused
docs: Emergency stop / resume (operator-only). Pauses value-in + payout paths; refund and
dispute-resolution paths remain open so consumer funds can never be trapped.

instructions.name: submit_completion
docs: Optimistic settlement step 1: the facilitator submits a completion with an
agent-committed `result_hash` and starts the dispute window. No funds move.

errors.name: InvalidFeeBps
errors.msg: Fee BPS exceeds 100%

errors.name: InvalidNodeCount
errors.msg: Node count must be between 1 and 16

errors.name: NodeAccountMismatch
errors.msg: Number of node accounts does not match node configs

errors.name: InvalidDag
errors.msg: Dependency graph contains a cycle or forward edge

errors.name: EmptyPipeline
errors.msg: Pipeline allocation must be greater than zero

errors.name: InvalidNodeAccount
errors.msg: Provided node account does not match expected PDA

errors.name: PipelineNotActive
errors.msg: Pipeline is not active

errors.name: NodeNotClaimable
errors.msg: Node is not in a claimable state

errors.name: DependenciesNotMet
errors.msg: Node dependencies are not all settled

errors.name: AgentMismatch
errors.msg: Agent does not match the stake account

errors.name: TierInsufficient
errors.msg: Agent tier is insufficient for this node

errors.name: UnauthorizedFacilitator
errors.msg: Caller is not the configured facilitator

errors.name: UnauthorizedArbiter
errors.msg: Caller is not the configured arbiter

errors.name: NodeNotSubmitted
errors.msg: Node is not in the Submitted state

errors.name: NodeNotDisputed
errors.msg: Node is not in the Disputed state

errors.name: DisputeWindowOpen
errors.msg: Dispute window is still open

errors.name: DisputeWindowClosed
errors.msg: Dispute window has closed

errors.name: NodeAlreadyDisputed
errors.msg: Node is already disputed

errors.name: NodeNotClaimed
errors.msg: Node is not claimed

errors.name: NodeNotExpirable
errors.msg: Node cannot be expired in its current state

errors.name: DeadlineNotPassed
errors.msg: Node deadline has not passed

errors.name: MissingSlashAccounts
errors.msg: Missing accounts required to slash a claimed node

errors.name: InvalidTreasury
errors.msg: Operator treasury account has wrong owner

errors.name: InvalidConsumerAccount
errors.msg: Consumer token account has wrong owner

errors.name: InvalidUri
errors.msg: Delivery URI length exceeds 96-byte buffer

errors.name: Paused
errors.msg: Protocol is paused

errors.name: InvalidDisputeWindow
errors.msg: Dispute window out of bounds

errors.name: NoPendingOperator
errors.msg: No pending operator to accept

errors.name: NotPendingOperator
errors.msg: Signer is not the pending operator

errors.name: AlreadyMigrated
errors.msg: Config already migrated

errors.name: PipelineHasActivity
errors.msg: Pipeline has claimed or settled nodes

errors.name: MathOverflow
errors.msg: Math overflow

fields.name: result_hash
docs: Agent-committed commitment to the delivered output (proof-of-delivery step).

types.name: NodeSettlement
docs: Companion account for the optimistic-settlement / dispute flow, created at
submit_completion and closed at finalize/resolve. Kept separate so PipelineNode
keeps its on-chain layout (no migration of existing nodes).

fields.name: result_hash
docs: sha256 of the delivered output bytes. Anyone can fetch `uri`, recompute
sha256, and prove a mismatch — this is what makes a dispute objective.

fields.name: uri
docs: Content-addressed retrieval pointer for the delivered output (IPFS CID,
Arweave id, or https URL). Fixed buffer keeps INIT_SPACE exact; `uri_len`
is the meaningful prefix length.

fields.name: dispute_slots
docs: Dispute window snapshotted from PipelineConfig at submit time, so an operator
cannot shorten an in-flight node's window out from under the consumer.

fields.name: version
docs: Layout version; 0 = pre-hardening (needs migrate), CONFIG_VERSION = current.

fields.name: paused
docs: Emergency stop: when true, value-in/payout instructions are blocked (refund +
dispute paths stay open).

fields.name: dispute_slots
docs: Operator-tunable dispute window (slots), snapshotted onto each NodeSettlement.

fields.name: pending_operator
docs: Two-step operator transfer target (default = none).

fields.name: max_slash_bps
docs: Hard ceiling on any single slash (per incident), caller-independent.

fields.name: pending_operator
docs: Two-step operator transfer target (default = none).
